questions: Does hlx tickets get correctly display approval status?
Does hlx tickets list correctly show an approval indicator?
Are there any issues with the CLI implementation?

answers.answer: Yes. printTicketDetail conditionally displays 'Approval: STATUS' when ticket.approvalStatus is truthy, following the exact mergeQueueStatus pattern at lines 58-60.
evidence: get.ts line 21: approvalStatus: string | null in TicketDetail type

answers.answer: Yes. The list output appends a [STATUS] tag when approvalStatus is non-null. When null, approvalTag is empty string and no indicator is shown.
evidence: list.ts line 11: approvalStatus: string | null in TicketItem type
list.ts line 107: approvalTag appended to console.log output

answers.answer: No issues found. Implementation follows existing patterns exactly, handles backward compatibility with older servers (undefined is falsy), and --json output automatically includes the field.
evidence: get.ts lines 58-63: mergeQueueStatus and approvalStatus use identical conditional pattern
list.ts lines 97-98: --json outputs raw server response

statement: CLI implementation reviewed and verified correct. No code changes needed. Both get and list commands correctly display approval status following existing patterns. TypeScript typecheck passes.
